title: Is A Corporate Body And Constituted ... vs Approached The Plaintiff Bank For ... on 30 May, 2022
body: Is A Corporate Body And Constituted ... vs Approached The Plaintiff Bank For ... on 30 May, 2022 IN THE COURT OF LXXXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, AT BENGALURU (CCH-86) (Commercial Court) THIS THE 30TH DAY OF MAY 2022 PRESENT: SMT. LATHAKUMARI M. M.A., LL.M., LXXXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BENGALURU. Com. O.S. No. 645/2022 BETWEEN: CANARA BANK Avenue Road Branch, No.105-110, Avenue Plaza, Avenue Road, Bangalore-560 002 By its Manager, Sri. Avinash Kumar, S/o Sri. Chandra Kishor Lal Das, Aged about 37 years, : PLAINTIFF (Represented by Sri. K.N. Mahabaleshwar Rao - Advocate) AND 1. M.K. FASHION FANCY DRESS, No.102, Avenue Plaza, 2 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 Avenue Road, Bengaluru -560 002, Represented by its Proprietor, Sri. Mukesh Kumar, 2. SRI. MUKESH KUMAR S/o Keval Chand, Aged about 25 years, Proprietor, M.K. FASHION FANCY DRESS, No. 102, Shop No.16, Avenue Plaza, Avenue Road, Bengaluru - 560 002. And, R/at C/o Balaji I Floor Single Room, Nethaji Nagar, 1st Cross, Magadi Road, Agrahara, Bangalore. And Also, Permanently R/at, Bilawas, Pali, Beelawas, State Rajasthan, PIN - 306 104. : DEFENDANTS (Ex-pate) Date of Institution of the suit 22.04.2022 Nature of the suit (suit on pronote, suit for declaration & Suit for recovery of money Possession, Suit for injunction etc.) 3 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 Date of commencement of ---- recording of evidence Date on which judgment was pronounced 30.05.2022 Total Duration Year/s Month/s Day/s 00 01 08 (LATHAKUMARI M.), LXXXV Addl.City Civil & Sessions Judge, Bengaluru. JUDGMENT This is a plaintiff's suit filed by Canara Bank seeking to pass a judgment and decree against the defendants jointly and severally for a sum of Rs.7,63,577.87 with court cost, current and future interest at the rate of 16.25% p.a., compounded monthly rests from the date of suit till realization and for such other reliefs as this court deems fit to grant in the facts and circumstances of this case. 2. The brief facts of the plaintiff's case is that, the plaintiff is a Corporate Body and constituted under the provisions of Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer 4 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 of Undertakings) Act, 1970 having its Head Office at J.C. Road, Bengaluru represented by its Chief Manager. It is plaintiff's further case that, the 1 st defendant is a Proprietorship concern run by its Proprietor 2 nd defendant herein carrying on business in the name and style of 'MK Fashion Fancy Dress' at No.102, Avenue Plaza, Avenue Road, Bengaluru. The business activity of defendant was to sell Ladies dress materials and apparels. It is plaintiff's further case that, 2nd defendant being the proprietor of 1 st defendant approached the plaintiff bank for financial assistance to carry on the business and submitted loan application requesting OD facility dated 24.07.2018. Considering the same, plaintiff's bank sanctioned loan of Rs.5,00,000/- on 09.08.2018 repayable along with interest at 10.75% p.a., and in case of default additional interest at the rate of rate of 2% p.a., shall be charged in respect of the said financial assistance which was granted for business purpose. Plaintiff's bank further mentioned that, at the time of availing the loan 1st defendant represented by 2nd defendant being the Proprietor has executed necessary loan documents in favour of plaintiff bank agreeing to repay the loan amount along with interest. When such being the state of affairs 2 nd defendant as a 5 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 proprietor of 1st defendant utilized all the loan amount and later became defaulter, has not paid the amount regularly towards loan account as agreed in terms of loan document executed by him. It is plaintiff's further case that, in spite of repeated requests and demand made by plaintiff's bank, defendants have not at all cleared the amount due i.e., outstanding amount due to the plaintiff bank. Though 2nd defendant executed letter of revival dated 18.02.2020 admitting his liability to clear the loan amount due to the plaintiff's bank, has not paid the amount towards the repayment of loan amount. Hence, plaintiff's bank got issued legal notice dated 29.08.2020 calling upon the defendants to make payment due along with interest. The notice issued to defendants returned with postal shara as 'no such person in the said address and also as insufficient address'. Even the notice sent by RPAD to Rajasthan address of the defendant returned with postal shara as 'no such person'. At para-8 of the plaint, Plaintiff's bank has mentioned that 2 nd defendant was holding credit card of plaintiff bank bearing No. 5162- 99021415-7005 and 2nd defendant was regularly using the said credit card. He is also having SB Account bearing No. 040210 1072355 in the plaintiff's branch. On account of 6 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 use of the credit card by 2nd defendant, the plaintiff bank has received bills for payment and debited the bills amounts from time to time to S.B. Account of 2 nd defendant. As such SB account of the 2 nd defendant was over drawn and showing credit debit balance from the month of October 2019. The 2nd defendant has not paid the credit card bill amount for having used the credit card. The defendants are due of Rs.7,63,577.87 towards the amount borrowed and credit card amount as on 21.04.2022 as per the regular accounts maintained by the plaintiff bank. Plaintiff's bank is entitled to recover the same along with interest at the rate of 16.25% per annum as agreed. Even the pre-mediation application filed by plaintiff's bank has been concluded without any settlement as the defendants failed to appear before the District Legal Services Authority. Hence, plaintiff constrained to file this suit for the reliefs mentioned supra. 3. On issuance of suit summons to the defendants through court and also RPAD to two separate addresses of defendants known to plaintiff's bank, summons were returned with postal shara as 'left the address' and 'no such firm' respectively. Considering the service of summons as deemed service, service held sufficient. 7 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 Since defendants were called out and remained absent, defendants placed ex-parte. 4. Since the plaint averments are in consistent with the original documents produced and relied upon by the plaintiff's bank, this court dispensed with plaintiff's evidence as per the provisions of Order VIII Rule 10 CPC . 5. I have carefully scrutinized the entire records placed before me. Heard the arguments. 6. Now the points that arise for my consideration are:- 1) Whether the Plaintiff's bank establishes that defendants are jointly and severally liable to pay Rs.7,63,577.87 to the plaintiff's bank along with interest at the rate of 16.25% per annum compounded monthly rests from the date of suit till realization with costs as claimed? 2) What Order? 7. My answers to the above Points are as under: Point No.1 :- In the Affirmative Point No.2 :- As per the final Order 8 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 for the following reasons. REASONS 8. Point No.1:- It is the specific case of plaintiff's bank that 1st defendant being the proprietorship concern and 2nd defendant being its Proprietor approached plaintiff's bank and availed loan facility to an extent of Rs.5,00,000/- which was sanctioned on 09.08.2018 with a condition that defendants shall repay the same along with future interest compounded monthly rests at the rate of 16.25% p.a. It is the specific contention of plaintiff's bank that defendants though utilized the entire loan amount failed to repay the same as per the terms and conditions mentioned in the loan documents. Apart from said loan, it is specifically mentioned that defendants i.e., 2 nd defendant was using the credit card facility pertaining to plaintiff's bank and he is also due of some amount towards said utilization of credit card facility in all amounting to Rs.7,63,577.87. To substantiate the same, plaintiff bank has produced as many as 10 documents. First document is the original loan application of OD facility dated 24.07.2018 submitted by 2 nd defendant as a Proprietor of 1st defendant. Document No.2 is the letter of proprietorship dated 09.08.2018. Document No.3 is the 9 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 OCC agreement dated 09.08.2018 executed by 2 nd defendant as a proprietor of 1st defendant. Document No.4 is the pronote executed by 2nd defendant on 09.08.2018 in favour of plaintiff's bank. Document No.5 is the letter of revival dated 18.02.2020. In pursuance of these documents, defendants as a proprietorship concern and proprietor respectively admits their liability towards plaintiff's bank as claimed. Apart from these documents, plaintiff bank has produced copy of legal notice and also returned RPAD covers issued to defendants with postal shara. Even defendants failed to appear before the District Legal Services Authority to work out settlement if any in this regard. The statement of account being the loan account and also SB account of defendants along with necessary certificate as required under Banker's Book of Evidence Act, 1891 establishes that defendants have committed default. Under such circumstances plaintiff's bank is at liberty to recover the amount due along with interest and also penal interest further with costs as claimed from defendants jointly and severally. That apart, the amount claimed by the plaintiff's bank is a public money. If defendants for any reason changed their place of residence or shop of premises, are under 10 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 obligation to inform the same to the plaintiff's bank. The amount available with plaintiff's bank is a State Exchequer money. The documents discussed supra establishes the plaintiff's claim against defendants. Accordingly, I answer Point No.1 in the Affirmative. 9. Point No.2 : - In view of my findings on Point No.1, I proceed to pass the following Order. ORDER The Suit filed by the plaintiff's bank for judgment and decree against the defendants for a sum of Rs.7,63,577.87 is decreed against defendants with cost jointly and severally. Defendants are liable to pay the same along with interest at the rate of 16.25 per cent per annum compounded monthly rests from the date of suit till realization. Defendants further directed to deposit the same within 30 11 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 days from today, failing which plaintiff's bank can recover the same from the defendants jointly and severally in accordance with law. Draw decree accordingly. (Dictated to the Judgment Writer, transcribed by him, corrected and then pronounced by me in open Court on this the 30th day of May, 2022). (LATHAKUMARI M.), LXXXV Addl. City Civil & Sessions Judge, Bengaluru. ANNEXURE LIST OF DOCUMENTS PRODUCED ON BEHALF OF THE PLAINTIFF ALONG WITH PLAINT (DEFENDANTS EX-PARTE) 1) Original Loan Application of OD facility dated 24.07.2018 2) Original Letter of Proprietorship dated 09.08.2018 3) Original OCC Agreement dated 09.08.2018 4) Original Pronote dated 09.08.2018 5) Original Letter of Revival dated 18.02.2020 12 Com. O.S. No.645/2022 6) Office Copy of the Notice dated 29.08.2020 7) Original 4-Un-served RPAD covers of Defendant Nos.1 and 2 8) Certified copy of Endorsement issued by District Legal Service Authority dated 23.07.2021 9) Xerox copy of Statement of Accounts bearing Loan A/c. No. 04022261030136 Along with Certificate under Section 2A (b) 2A (c) of The Banker's Book Evidence act, 1891 (as amended). 10) Xerox copy of Statement of Accounts bearing S.B. A/c. No. 0402101072355 Along with Certificate under Section 2A (b) 2A (c) of The Banker's Book Evidence act, 1891 (as amended). LIST OF WITNESSES EXAMINED ON BEHALF OF THE DEFENDANTS NIL LIST OF DOCUMENTS EXHIBITED ON BEHALF OF THE DEFENDANTS NIL (LATHAKUMARI M.), LXXXV Addl.City Civil & Sessions Judge, Bengaluru.
